response.justification: The candidate is an experienced full-stack and systems software developer with roughly 15 years of experience, which technically satisfies the bachelor's-plus-8-years path. However, his background is almost entirely web, mobile, and backend product engineering, and the role demands specialized hardware expertise: SoC and IP modeling, AI accelerator performance analysis, GPU architecture, and hardware-software co-design. Nothing in the resume evidences deep-learning workload optimization, SIMD/MIMD architecture work, or silicon-adjacent performance modeling. His low-latency DLang work and brief C/C++ tooling experience are the closest touchpoints, but they fall far short of principal-level performance-architecture requirements.
strengths: Long, continuous engineering career (15+ years) across web, backend, and some embedded work, exceeding the raw experience requirement
Genuine systems performance exposure at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic: low-latency DLang services with async I/O, fibers, and caching at millions of requests per second
Strong software craftsmanship signals — open-source authorship, testing frameworks, TDD advocacy, and public talks/workshops
concerns: No SoC/IP modeling, hardware architecture, or hardware-software co-design experience anywhere in the resume
No AI/deep-learning workload, GPU architecture, or SIMD/MIMD background — the core preferred and required skills are absent
Career trajectory is senior product/full-stack engineering, not the principal-level hardware performance-architecture track this role requires

key_factors.factor: Missing SoC/IP modeling and hardware co-design experience
No AI accelerator, GPU, or deep-learning workload expertise
